lessons.title: Community Call — Jul 20, 2026
lessons.markdown:
# Community Call — Jul 20, 2026

Samin discusses optimizing AI model usage by leveraging Codex and Hermes for cost-effective, high-volume tasks. He provides a framework for choosing between Claude, ChatGPT/Codex, and Grok based on their specific strengths and explains how to implement a 'squad of agents' for specialized workflows. Samin and Danny demonstrate a new community MCP tool that allows members to query past call transcripts and course materials directly within their AI agents. They also discuss the practical trade-offs between using proprietary models like Grok and GPT versus local or open-source models like DeepSeek and Kimi, emphasizing efficiency and token cost. Samin demonstrates a new 'Ask Samin' skill that allows members to query Claude Club content directly within their own workflows using MCP. The discuss

Topics: AI Model Optimization, Codex, Hermes, Agentic Workflows, Cost Management, MCP, Knowledge Management, Model Selection, AI Agents, Community Resources, Ask Samin, Content Creation, Workflow Automation, Community Tools, Payment Processing, AI Workflows, Tool Subscriptions, Agent Club, Community Navigation, Recording Workflows, Script Debugging, Automation, Cold Calling, Sales Strategy, GoHighLevel, AI Voice Agents, Performance-based Pricing, offer-refinement, performance-pricing, lead-generation

## Knowledge from this call

### 1. Optimizing AI Costs and Model Selection (02:06)
**Member asked:** How do you get so much usage out of your subscriptions, and why is Codex preferred over Claude for certain tasks?
**Samin:** Samin explains that by using Codex with Hermes, he can leverage subscription-based token limits that reset frequently, effectively providing near-unlimited usage for a flat fee. He contrasts this with Claude, which often requires pay-per-token API usage that can become expensive. He suggests using Claude for creative tasks, ChatGPT/Codex for precise execution of instructions, and Grok for speed and cost-efficiency.

Tools: Codex, Hermes, Claude, ChatGPT, Grok

When to use: use when a member asks about managing AI costs or choosing the right model for a task
### 2. Routing Tasks to Specific Models (08:03)
**Member asked:** Can I configure an agent to automatically route specific tasks to different models like Grok or Codex?
**Samin:** Yes, this can be achieved through 'mixture of agents' or by creating a 'squad of agents' in Multica. Samin demonstrates using system prompts to assign specific roles, such as designating Codex as the 'execution agent' within a squad, while noting that 90% of users should focus on mastering one model and agentic harness before attempting complex routing.

Tools: Hermes, Codex, Grok, Multica

When to use: use when a member asks about advanced agent routing or model switching
### 3. Model Strengths Framework (06:04)
Samin provides a rule of thumb for model selection: Claude is best for creativity and design; ChatGPT/Codex acts like a 'rottweiler' that excels at following precise instructions; Grok is the go-to for speed and cost-effectiveness.

Tools: Claude, ChatGPT, Codex, Grok

When to use: use when a member asks which model to use for a specific type of work
### 4. Community Knowledge Retrieval Tool (12:28)
Samin introduces a new tool being built for Claude Club members that connects to their AI (Claude, ChatGPT, or Hermes) to search through past community call recordings and course materials. This allows members to ask questions like 'What is Samin's opinion on X?' and receive direct links to the relevant resources.

Tools: Claude, ChatGPT, Hermes, MCP
### 5. Demo — Installing and Using the Community MCP Skill (15:02)
Danny demonstrates how to install the community MCP skill by pasting a provided link into Claude. The tool is designed as a bundle that pulls from a centralized database, ensuring that even if a member has already installed the skill, they receive the latest updates to the community knowledge base automatically.

Tools: Claude, MCP
### 6. Model Efficiency and Real-World Usage (17:35)
**Member asked:** Why does Samin prefer proprietary models like GPT or Grok over open-source or Chinese models like DeepSeek and Kimi?
**Samin:** Samin argues that proprietary models are more efficient in terms of 'dollars per token' and speed. He notes that Kimi is token-inefficient, making it costly, and that running local models requires significant hardware investment and maintenance overhead (e.g., re-downloading models on updates) that outweighs the benefits for most users.

Tools: Grok, GPT, DeepSeek, Kimi
### 7. Demo — Using the Ask Samin Skill (25:03)
Samin demonstrates how to invoke the 'Ask Samin' skill via slash commands or direct prompts. He shows how to install it as a custom connector using an MCP URL in settings, allowing the model to pull specific, context-aware answers and links from Claude Club content rather than providing generic AI advice.

Tools: MCP, Claude

When to use: use when a member wants to know how to set up or use the Ask Samin skill to get specific community-based answers
### 8. Content Creation and Hermes (30:23)
**Member asked:** How can I create content like carousels for specific niches?
**Samin:** Samin suggests using Hermes to generate content or setting up Hermes with Higgsfield to automate the production of assets like carousels.

Tools: Hermes, Higgsfield

When to use: use when a member asks about automating content creation or using Hermes for specific formats
### 9. Payment Processing Challenges (36:16)
**Member asked:** How can I handle payments for a sensitive product (psilocybin) when traditional processors are difficult to secure and crypto is associated with scams?
**Samin:** Samin acknowledges the difficulty of the situation and asks if the member has already reached out to the two specific companies previously recommended in the community.

When to use: use when a member asks about high-risk payment processing or business infrastructure
### 10. Payment Processor Recommendations (37:24)
**Member asked:** Which payment processors should I use for my product?
**Samin:** Samin recommends Whop, FanBasis, and Authorize.Net. He notes that these processors often require information about the 'labs' or specific product details to approve accounts.

When to use: use when a member asks about setting up payments for their product
### 11. Demo — Interior Design Workflow with ChatGPT (39:05)
Samin demonstrates taking a photo of a room and uploading it to ChatGPT. He asks the AI to decorate the space and find links to products that match the visual style, providing a practical workflow for interior design.

Tools: ChatGPT

When to use: use when a member wants a creative, non-coding use case for AI
### 12. Hermes Course and Tool Usage (40:25)
**Member asked:** Should I finish the full course before starting Hermes, and how should I manage subscriptions?
**Samin:** Samin explains that the Hermes course is highly practical for content creation, so members can start it immediately. He advises against paying for Hermes directly; instead, configure it to use ChatGPT or Codex providers in the settings to avoid unnecessary costs. He suggests sticking to the standard $20/month subscriptions for ChatGPT or Claude as they offer the best value for tokens.

Tools: Hermes, ChatGPT, Codex, Claude

When to use: use when a member is confused about course order or subscription costs
### 13. Agent Club Relevance (47:40)
**Member asked:** Should I be using Agent Club?
**Samin:** Samin advises that Agent Club is an advanced tool for users who have already mastered individual tools like Claude Code, Codex, and Hermes. He suggests focusing on the fundamentals and completing the Hermes course first before moving to centralized dashboard tools.

Tools: Claude Code, Codex, Hermes

When to use: use when a member asks about advanced orchestration tools
### 14. Finding Call Recordings (50:08)
**Member asked:** Where can I find the previous call recordings?
**Samin:** Samin explains that members can use the search bar in Claude Club for 'call recording' or scroll to the bottom of the classroom section to find the 'Master Class and Call Recordings' area.

Tools: Claude Club

When to use: use when a member asks how to find past community call recordings
### 15. Improving Knowledge Base Searchability (50:53)
Samin discusses building an MCP tool that allows members to query the call recordings directly, enabling them to search for specific questions asked by members to track progress and retrieve historical context.

Tools: MCP

When to use: use when discussing the future of the Claude Club knowledge base and search capabilities
### 16. Recording Platform Strategy (51:51)
Samin explores moving calls to Zoom to bypass Skool's limitations on meeting recorders. He notes that while the platform choice changes, the goal remains to keep the knowledge base fresh and avoid answering the same questions repeatedly, while acknowledging that re-asking questions can be useful for capturing updated AI capabilities.

When to use: use when discussing the logistics of community calls and recording tools
### 17. Debugging Background Script Pop-ups (55:36)
**Member asked:** How can I identify which of my background trading watchers is causing random pop-up windows?
**Samin:** Samin suggests creating a 'master watcher' script that traverses the file tree of the user's projects. He advises using Codex to audit the existing scripts, generate a report identifying which ones trigger pop-ups, and then deciding whether to disable or modify them.

Tools: Codex

When to use: use when a member has multiple background scripts causing UI interference and needs a way to audit them
### 18. Tools for Cold Calling Feedback (~62:00)
**Member asked:** What is the best power dialer or tool to get AI summaries and feedback on cold calls?
**Samin:** Samin suggests looking into Gong.io or Close.io for sales training and feedback. He also notes that HubSpot is a viable, cost-effective option that includes dialers and AI summaries, and advises against over-complicating the tech stack.

When to use: use when a member asks for recommendations on cold calling software or tools to improve sales performance.
### 19. Reframing the Sales Offer (~66:00)
Samin explains that selling 'voice and SMS agents' is a weak offer because clients don't know how to use them. Instead, frame the offer around solving revenue-generating problems, such as increasing the percentage of customers returning for inspections. Use leading questions to uncover gaps in their current process rather than trying to replace their existing systems.

When to use: use when a member is struggling to get clients or is focusing too much on the technical features of their AI service.
### 20. Avoiding Technical Rabbit Holes (~70:22)
Samin warns against obsessing over specific voice AI providers (like ElevenLabs vs. Vapi) because the marginal gains in voice quality are small and don't significantly impact results. He emphasizes that SMS often drives 80% of the performance lift and that the focus should remain on the business outcome, not the underlying tech.

When to use: use when a member is worried about voice quality objections or comparing competing AI voice platforms.
### 21. Structuring Performance-Based Offers (~73:45)
Samin advises reorienting offers to provide meaningful business value by pricing based on the percentage lift in new business generated. He emphasizes that for the first client, the goal is not scalability but simply securing a partner to prove the model, recommending warm outreach over cold calling.

When to use: use when a member is struggling to define their service offer or how to price their work for new clients
### 22. Member-to-Member Collaboration (~75:43)
**Member asked:** A member offers a potential partnership to another member to solve a lead-response issue in the healthcare industry.
**Samin:** A member identifies a pain point in their healthcare business where incoming leads are lost due to slow response times. They propose a collaboration to implement better automated follow-up systems. Samin encourages this connection, noting the importance of HIPAA compliance in that sector and directing them to use the community resources and technical support team for implementation.

When to use: use when members are looking for real-world business opportunities or partnerships within the community

## Source

Open this Claude Club call: https://www.skool.com/claude/call-recording-jul-20th

lessons.summary:
Samin discusses optimizing AI model usage by leveraging Codex and Hermes for cost-effective, high-volume tasks. He provides a framework for choosing between Claude, ChatGPT/Codex, and Grok based on their specific strengths and explains how to implement a 'squad of agents' for specialized workflows. Samin and Danny demonstrate a new community MCP tool that allows members to query past call transcripts and course materials directly within their AI agents. They also discuss the practical trade-offs between using proprietary models like Grok and GPT versus local or open-source models like DeepSeek and Kimi, emphasizing efficiency and token cost.
